feat: community channels route to shared 'Community' category

Unverified subrappters are their own thing — they don't collapse into
General. All posts to unverified channels route to the 'Community'
catch-all Discussions category while preserving channel metadata.

When admin creates a dedicated category matching the slug, the channel
auto-graduates to verified and posts route directly there.

Changes:
- state_io.py: new resolve_category_id() — channel → community → general
- zion_autonomy.py: 3 call sites use resolve_category_id()
- content_engine.py: 1 call site uses resolve_category_id()
- channel.py: already defaults to verified: false (previous commit)
- CONSTITUTION.md: clarified community channels are standalone entities

TODO: Create 'Community' Discussions category in GitHub repo settings.
Until then, falls back to 'general' gracefully.

fix: correct VM pricing to use PAYG Linux/Windows rates only

The Azure Retail Prices API returns multiple items per SKU including Spot,
Low Priority, and Cloud Services variants — all with type='Consumption'.
The previous filter only excluded Windows, causing Spot/Low Priority prices
to be selected (e.g., Standard_B2als_v2 showed \.89 instead of \.51).

Changes:
- get_vm_pricing(): explicitly selects PAYG 'Virtual Machines' Linux item
  (excludes Spot, Low Priority, Cloud Services); also fetches Windows price
- get_vm_pricing() returns both Linux and Windows PAYG prices
- refresh_region(): stores hourlyPriceUSDWindows/monthlyPriceUSDWindows in cache
- get_vm_skus_with_cache(): passes Windows pricing through in SKU dicts
- get_sku_from_cache(): reconstructs pricing dict from entity fields (adds Windows)
- New admin/refresh-region HTTP endpoint: POST to manually re-cache a region
  without waiting for the next daily timer run
- Frontend: Linux/Windows OS pricing toggle above results table
- Frontend: CSV export includes both Linux and Windows pricing columns

[feat](engine): Polly resilience pipeline for CapitalComService

- Added retry (3 attempts, exponential backoff) + circuit breaker to CapitalComService
- Handles 429 TooManyRequests, 5xx server errors, HttpRequestException, transient timeouts
- Circuit breaker opens after 50% failure rate in 30s window (5+ requests), pauses 30s
- Applied to GetHistoricalPricesAsync, GetPositionsAsync, SearchMarketsAsync, GetMarketSnapshotAsync
- Replaced manual 429 retry loop in GetHistoricalPrices with Polly pipeline
- Uses existing Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Resilience (Polly v8) dependency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Persist Mp3tag config across container restarts via volume mount

- Seed /pw/initial/config with default Mp3tag AppData at build time
- On startup, entrypoint copies seed to /mp3tag-web/config if not present
- Symlink Wine AppData/Roaming/Mp3tag -> /mp3tag-web/config so Mp3tag
  reads/writes config through the host-mounted ./data/config/ directory
- Settings (columns, tag formats, window state, etc.) now survive restarts

Fix stack overflow in AI destructor chain: use qualified ~AIModule() calls

Root cause: Classes like ConstructionAIModule, DiplomacyAIModule,
EmotionalAIModule, ResearchAIModule, and ResourceAIModule share
AIModule's memory layout via padding fields but don't inherit from it.
Their destructors called ((AIModule*)this)->~AIModule() which is a
VIRTUAL call through the vtable — dispatching back to the current
class's destructor, causing infinite recursion and stack overflow.

Fix: Use qualified calls (AIModule::~AIModule()) to bypass virtual
dispatch. Same fix applied to ConstructionItem::~ConstructionItem()
which had identical issue with BaseItem::~BaseItem().

Also removed temporary SEH wrappers from TribeMainDecisionAIModule
that were added during investigation.

Verified: 3 clean shutdown cycles with ExitCode=0, no exceptions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix window visibility: set xvfb to 1280x720 so xpra workspace appears in viewport

Without this, xpra placed the virtual workspace at X11 (1200,640) in the

which put the entire window off-screen in the browser.

the display, resulting in CSS left:183px/top:0px — fully visible in viewport.
